Definition.text: Increased is an additive modifier token applied to a stat or stat group as a percentage sum.

Purpose.text: Accumulate additive contributions before multiplicative buckets are applied.

Core Rules.text: All Increased terms resolve additively within the same stage; a single value is formed by summing positive and negative percentage contributions in that bucket.

Internal Rules.text: Increased entries are stored in an additive accumulator and applied before More/Less multipliers.

Calculation Order.text: 1) base value 2) aggregate Increased/Reduced pool 3) apply More/Less multipliers 4) enforce caps.

Priority Rules.text: Mechanic-specific text can constrain or scope where increased bonuses apply, overriding generic bucket assumptions.

Preconditions.text: A stat must be eligible for additive scaling and expose an Increased-compatible modifier source.

Postconditions.text: The stat receives an additive modifier to its base stage before multiplicative scaling.

Limitations.text: Increased is not equivalent to More/Less and must not be merged into multiplicative factors.

Caps.text: No universal Added or Reduced cap is defined for Increased alone; target systems define hard limits.

Formula.text: final = base * (1 + Σ Increased_delta_i + Σ Reduced_delta_j) * Π More_k * Π (1 - Less_l)

Conflicts.text: Reduced and Increased are often mixed in text examples but remain separate symbols for explicit language constructs.

Exceptions.text: Some explicit support-given modifiers apply to a strict subset of hits/skills and are not globally additive.

Hidden Rules.text: Store Increased deltas as additive list elements to preserve patch-level changes to stack behavior.

Developer Notes.text: Do not encode Increased as More with inverted sign; bucket ordering matters for downstream scaling.

Historical Changes.text: No official version break recorded in this pass; additive semantics for Increased remain active in captured scope.

text.note: Increased remains additive in the captured mechanic model.

text: 10% Increased and 20% Reduced combine as -10% in additive stage.
15% Increased and 30% Increased sum to +45% before multiplicative stage.
Increased and Reduced are equivalent to More/Less

overrides: Reduced in same additive bucket semantics
